perturbation.original_text: 4.2.3 In addition to the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee and Franchise Sales Royalty Fee described above, you shall collect from each Unit Franchisee in your Master Territory a "National Advertising Fund Contribution" to be contributed to our "National Advertising Fund" pursuant to the terms of the individual Unit Franchise Agreements. For each Unit Franchise you own and operate, you shall pay the National Advertising Fund Contribution on the same basis as Unit Franchisees. The National Advertising Fund Contribution shall be collected by you from each Unit Franchisee and spent by you in accordance with the agreement on behalf of the franchisor and Franchisees.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2.3 In addition to the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee and Franchise Sales Royalty Fee described above, you shall collect from each Unit Franchisee in your Master Territory a "National Advertising Fund Contribution" to be contributed to our "National Advertising Fund" pursuant to the terms of the individual Unit Franchise Agreements. For each Unit Franchise you own and operate, you shall pay the National Advertising Fund Contribution on the same basis as Unit Franchisees. The National Advertising Fund Contribution shall be collected by you from each Unit Franchisee and contributed according to the terms outlined in Exhibit Z.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the 'National Advertising Fund Contribution' should be spent in accordance with the agreement on behalf of the franchisor and Franchisees. However, the modified text indicates that these contributions will be done according to the terms outlined in 'Exhibit Z', but this exhibit does not exist, this creates an in-text contradiction, as the agreement is being referred to an Exhibit that does not exist, causing uncertainty about the application and amount to be payed to this concept. This is a contradiction because the contract refers to a non-existent term of contribution, so the previous agreement clause is not being enforced, contradicting the text within section 4.2.3
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2.3 In addition to the Unit Franchise Performance Royalty Fee and Franchise Sales Royalty Fee described above, you shall collect from each Unit Franchisee in your Master Territory a "National Advertising Fund Contribution" to be contributed to our "National Advertising Fund" pursuant to the terms of the individual Unit Franchise Agreements. For each Unit Franchise you own and operate, you shall pay the National Advertising Fund Contribution on the same basis as Unit Franchisees. The National Advertising Fund Contribution shall be collected by you from each Unit Franchisee and contributed according to the terms outlined in Exhibit Z.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.2.3 replaces the original instruction of how the 'National Advertising Fund Contribution' should be spent (in accordance with the agreement) with a reference to 'Exhibit Z'. Since Exhibit Z does not exist, this creates a contradiction because the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) refers to a non-existent term of contribution, effectively nullifying the initial agreement clause and creating uncertainty about the contribution's application. This adheres to the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language is altered, creating a conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 Insurance During the term of this Agreement, you shall maintain in force under policies of insurance issued by licensed insurers approved by us insurance coverage as we from time to time require. You must maintain insurance related to your operation of the Franchised Business. Such insurance coverage will include: 5.5.1 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: broad form comprehensive general liability coverage against claims for employment practices coverage, bodily and personal injury, death and property damage caused by or occurring in conjunction with the conduct of business by you pursuant to this Agreement and broad form contractual liability coverage, including errors and omissions coverage, under one or more policies of insurance containing minimum liability coverage prescribed by us from time to time, but in no event in an amount less than Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) aggregate. Such insurance shall not have a deductible or self-insured retention in excess of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000); 5.5.2 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: automobile liability insurance coverage, including owned and non-owned vehicles, with limits of not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) per occurrence;
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 Insurance During the term of this Agreement, you shall maintain in force insurance coverage as we from time to time require. You must maintain insurance related to your operation of the Franchised Business. Such insurance coverage will include: 5.5.1 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: broad form comprehensive general liability coverage against claims for employment practices coverage, bodily and personal injury, death and property damage caused by or occurring in conjunction with the conduct of business by you pursuant to this Agreement and broad form contractual liability coverage, including errors and omissions coverage. Such insurance shall not have a deductible or self-insured retention in excess of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000); 5.5.2 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: automobile liability insurance coverage, including owned and non-owned vehicles, with limits of not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000) per occurrence;
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the insurance should be issued by licensed insurers approved by us, but the omission of the phrase "issued by licensed insurers approved by us" creates an in-text contradiction. It becomes unclear whether the franchisee can choose any insurer or if there are specific requirements, this uncertainty creates a contradiction within the section. The location 5.5 is now contradicting the location 5.5.1 because it is ommitting part of the definition of the previous clause, which is part of the same location. It is affecting the enforceability of the following insurance requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5.1 As it relates to the operation of your Franchised Business: broad form comprehensive general liability coverage against claims for employment practices coverage, bodily and personal injury, death and property damage caused by or occurring in conjunction with the conduct of business by you pursuant to this Agreement and broad form contractual liability coverage, including errors and omissions coverage, under one or more policies of insurance containing minimum liability coverage prescribed by us from time to time, but in no event in an amount less than Two Million Dollars ($2,000,000) aggregate.
perturbation.justification: The omission of "issued by licensed insurers approved by us" in Section 5.5 creates a contradiction because the original text implies that the insurance must be from approved insurers, while the changed text does not specify this requirement. This contradicts the requirement outlined in Section 5.5.1 where the insurer needs to meet our minimum liability coverage, therefore, by removing the obligation from 5.5, it makes it unclear as to whether 5.5.1 is enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You shall expend not less than $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising. To protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill in the industry, you must submit samples of all proposed Solicitation Advertising materials to us at least fifteen (15) days before any use of the same. If we do not disapprove the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the same, you may use the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials as submitted to us; provided, however, that if such Solicitation Advertising is required to be submitted to a government agency, you shall so submit such materials to the applicable government agency and shall not use such materials until the materials are approved or disapproved or if the use of the materials otherwise become permissible under law, such as if notice of disapproval is not received from a governmental agency within a stated period of time prescribed by law. We may, at any time after you begin to use the Solicitation Advertising materials, prohibit further use of the same, effective upon your receipt of written notice from us to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You shall expend not less than $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising. To protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill in the industry, you must submit samples of all proposed Solicitation Advertising materials to us. If we do not disapprove the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the same, you may use the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials as submitted to us; provided, however, that if such Solicitation Advertising is required to be submitted to a government agency, you shall so submit such materials to the applicable government agency and shall not use such materials until the materials are approved or disapproved or if the use of the materials otherwise become permissible under law, such as if notice of disapproval is not received from a governmental agency within a stated period of time prescribed by law. We may, at any time after you begin to use the Solicitation Advertising materials, prohibit further use of the same, effective upon your receipt of written notice from us to do so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the franchisee must submit samples of advertising materials at least fifteen (15) days before use. By removing 'at least fifteen (15) days before any use of the same' creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction. If the submission is not required, there are no clear guidelines or terms about the advertising the franchisee is doing, the new clause now directly contradicts the first sentence of the paragraph. So the Franchisee could argue that the submission can be done whenever he wants, affecting the entire enforceability of the clause. The paragraph is contradictory at its core
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.14 Solicitation Advertising You shall conduct advertising to solicit Applicants for the purchase of Unit Franchisees in the Master Territory ("Solicitation Advertising"). You shall expend not less than $5,000 each month on such Solicitation Advertising. To protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill in the industry, you must submit samples of all proposed Solicitation Advertising materials to us. If we do not disapprove the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials within fifteen (15) days after receipt of the same, you may use the proposed Solicitation Advertising materials as submitted to us; provided, however, that if such Solicitation Advertising is required to be submitted to a government agency, you shall so submit such materials to the applicable government agency and shall not use such materials until the materials are approved or disapproved or if the use of the materials otherwise become permissible under law, such as if notice of disapproval is not received from a governmental agency within a stated period of time prescribed by law. We may, at any time after you begin to use the Solicitation Advertising materials, prohibit further use of the same, effective upon your receipt of written notice from us to do so.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'at least fifteen (15) days before any use of the same' creates a contradiction within section 5.14 itself. While the franchisee still needs to submit the advertising materials, the lack of a specific timeframe for submission introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the initial requirement to submit materials to 'protect the Proprietary Marks and our goodwill'. The contradicted text is within the Changed Text itself since it's the same section. This makes the entire clause less enforceable and creates internal inconsistency.
